page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 96 dedicated physical cores based on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and includes a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. No local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark physics tests scoring in the top 10% of all servers. Memory subsystem performance is also in the top tier, showing exceptional read and write bandwidth alongside high-speed Passmark memory and database operations. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average and strong categories, respectively.

The server exhibits distinct performance profiles for large language model inference. While text generation for medium (7B) and large (70B) models ranks in the top tier, text generation for small (135M) models is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. This indicates a resource density optimized for larger, memory-bound model weights rather than low-latency execution of smaller models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8gb.24xlarge offers high memory density and high-bandwidth networking without the added cost of local storage or GPU accelerators. This profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale database operations, Redis in-memory caching, multi-threaded software compilation, and medium-to-large LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.24xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 768.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory ratio, and provides 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmark results place its multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations in the top tier, while single-core metrics remain average to strong. Large language model text generation for 7B and 70B models is highly efficient, though text generation for 135M models is weak. This hardware profile is optimized for memory-intensive applications, including in-memory databases like Redis, large-scale data processing, multi-threaded software compilation, and medium-to-large LLM inference workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.24xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server with 96 dedicated Graviton cores, 768 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized platform featuring ninety-six dedicated cores and high-bandwidth networking for memory-intensive database and enterprise workloads.

768.0 GB of system memory with a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for Redis caching, database operations, and medium-to-large LLM inference
